description: Disclosure is provided on when an AI system is being used and the involvement of an AI system in decision-making, what kind of data it uses, and its purpose. The reasoning behind an AI system's decision is communicated in a way that is understandable to a range of people, as it is not always clear how an AI system has arrived at a conclusion.

subprinciples.description: Deployers have a responsibility to clearly disclose the implementation of an AI system to stakeholders and foster general awareness of the AI system being used.

subprinciples.subprinciple: Disclosure of Human-AI Interaction Context & Behaviour
subprinciples.description: Users have the right to know when and how AI systems interact with users and the expected behaviour of the AI systems.
The deployer should provide explainations to the users on how AI systems make decisions.
Documenting the repeatability of results produced by the AI system.

subprinciples.subprinciple: Ensuring Traceability in AI System Development and Decision Making Process
subprinciples.description: Ensuring traceability by building an audit trail to document the AI system development and decision making process.
Facilitating auditability by keeping a comprehensive record of data provenance, procurement, pre-processing, lineage, storage, and security.

Model cards are short documents that accompany trained machine learning models, disclosing the context in which the models are intended to be used, details of the performance evaluation procedures, and other relevant information.

description: Deployers should have safeguards in place to ensure that algorithmic decisions do not further exacerbate or amplify existing discriminatory or unjust impacts across different demographics and the design, development, and deployment of AI systems should not result in unfair biasness or discrimination.

subprinciples.description: Algorithmic decisions do not further exacerbate or amplify existing discriminatory or unjust impacts across different demographics, and the design,development, and deployment of AI systems should not result in unfair bias or discrimination.

subprinciples.subprinciple: Lifecycle Alignment with Fairness & Equity Principles
subprinciples.description: It is important that the design, development, and deployment of AI systems align with fairness and equity principles.
Appropriate measures should be taken to mitigate potential biases during data collection and pre-processing, training, and inference.

description: The safety of developers, deployers, and users of AI systems are ensured by conducting impact or risk assessments and ensuring that known risks have been identified and mitigated. The cybersecurity of AI systems is ensured, including mechanisms against malicious attacks specific to AIs, such as data poisoning, model inversion, the tampering of datasets, byzantine attacks in federated learning, as well as other attacks designed to reverse engineer personal data used to train the AI.

subprinciples.description: A risk prevention approach should be adopted, and precautions should be put in place so that humans can intervene to prevent harm.
The system can safely disengage itself in the event that an AI system makes unsafe decisions. Autonomous vehicles that cause injury to pedestrians are an illustration of this.

Before deploying AI systems, deployers should conduct risk assessments and relevant testing or certification and implement the appropriate level of human intervention to prevent harm when unsafe decisions are made.

subprinciples.subprinciple: Disclosure of Risks, Limitations, and Safeguards

description: AI systems respect human-centered values and pursue benefits for human society, well-being, nutrition, happiness,etc.

subprinciples.description: Human-centricity should be incorporated throughout the AI system lifecycle, starting from the design to development and deployment.

subprinciples.subprinciple: Prevention of Manipulative or Malicious Use
subprinciples.description: AI systems should not be used for malicious purposes or to sway or deceive users into making decisions that are not beneficial to them or society.

As an extension of human-centricity as a principle, it is also important to ensure that the adoption of AI systems and their deployment at scale do not unduly disrupt labor and job prospects without proper assessment.

description: AI systems have proper mechanisms in place to ensure data privacy and protection, and maintain and protect the quality and integrity of data throughout their entire lifecycle.

subprinciples.description: Data privacy and protection should be respected and upheld during the design, development, and deployment of AIsystems.The way data is collected, stored, generated, and deleted throughout the AIsystem lifecycle must comply with applicable data protection laws, data governance legislation, and ethical principles.
Organisations should be transparent about their data collection practices, including the types of data collected, how it is used, and who has access to it.
These frameworks should also be periodically reviewed and updated in accordance with applicable privacy and data protection laws.

Developers and deployers of AI systems should also incorporate a privacy by design principle when developing and deploying AI systems. Privacy by design is an approach that embeds privacy in every stage of the system development lifecycle.

Another consideration is investing in privacy-enhancing technologies to preserve privacy while allowing personal data to be used for innovation. Privacy enhancing technologies include, but are not limited to, differential privacy and zero-knowledge proofs.

description: Deployers should be able to account for decisions made by AI systems and for the compliance with applicable laws and respect for AI ethics and principles. AI actors should act with integrity throughout the AI system lifecycle when designing, developing, and deploying AI systems.

subprinciples.description: Deployers must ensure AI systems function properly and comply with laws, governance policies, and ethics. If malfunctions or misuse occur and cause harm, responsible individuals must act with integrity, take corrective measures, and implement safeguards to prevent reocurrence.

Organizations must set up clear internal governance frameworks with well-defined reporting lines and explicit allocation of roles and responsibilities across the AI lifecycle (design, development, deployment, monitoring).
AI systems should be built and deployed with integrity, and when errors or unethical outcomes occur, they must at minimum be documented and corrected to prevent harm to users.

description: A system should be sufficiently robust to cope with errors during execution and unexpected or erroneous input, or cope with stressful environmental conditions. It should also perform consistently. AI systems should, where possible, work reliably and have consistent results for a range of inputs and situations.

subprinciples.description: AI systems should be designed to handle real-world, dynamic conditions where inputs may change rapidly. They must remain resilient to unexpected data, avoid dangerous behaviors, and continue to fulfill their intended purpose.

subprinciples.subprinciple: Access Control & Safeguards for Critical Systems
subprinciples.description: Deployers should enforce strong access controls and protections for critical or sensitive AI systems, and take corrective actions when negative outcomes arise from unreliable performance.
Deployers must conduct comprehensive testing before deployment to ensure robustness and consistent performance across a wide range of scenarios and environments.
